unit: Unit 12: Everyday Life with Your Computer
title: Notes and Documents
drDigitalIntro:
The Notes app is perfect for quick ideas, lists, and reminders.

Try it on your own computer:

1. Open the Notes app (look for it in your apps or search for "Notes").
2. Click New Note (or the + button).
3. Type a shopping list:
   Milk
   Eggs
   Bread
   Butter
4. Notice it saves automatically — no need to click Save.
5. Close the app and open it again — your note is still there!

Notes sync across your devices. If you write a note on your computer, it appears on your phone too (as long as you're signed in to the same account).

For longer writing like letters, reports, or school work, use a word processor like Google Docs (free in your browser) or Microsoft Word.
drDigitalSuccess: Notes are saved automatically and sync across all your devices. Use Notes for quick thoughts, and Google Docs or Word for longer documents.
drDigitalHint: Open the Notes app on your computer and try creating a new note.
